ACT participants overwhelmingly attend postsecondary (62.6% vs. 27.2%), illustrating a sharp divide between direct work and education pathways【554†source】.

**5 Years After Graduation:** Regional meaningful employment is still higher among non‑ACT students (28.7% vs. 25.0%), but ACT takers gain ground in **statewide meaningful employment (17.8% vs. 11.3%)**. Postsecondary attendance is notably higher among ACT students (21.1% vs. 8.6%). Disconnection remains higher for non‑ACT (26.6% vs. 17.2%)【555†source】.

**10 Years After Graduation:** Regional meaningful employment narrows (29.9% non‑ACT vs. 28.6% ACT). ACT takers surpass non‑takers in **statewide meaningful employment (24.2% vs. 13.9%)**, while non‑ACT students remain more tied to regional employment. Disconnection is still higher for non‑ACT students (33.7% vs. 31.3%). The long‑term trajectory suggests that **ACT takers shift into broader statewide employment, while non‑takers retain a stronger regional employment profile**【556†source】.

::: panel-tabset
### One year after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments took.ACT one year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.1, took.ACT) %>%
  filter(grad.year.1 != "After 2023") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.1, var.col = data$took.ACT, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE,
         file = "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/took-ACT-1.html")
```

### Five years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments took.ACT five year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.5, took.ACT) %>%
  filter(grad.year.5 != "After 2023") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.5, var.col = data$took.ACT, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE,
         file = "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/took-ACT-5.html")
```

### Ten years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments took.ACT ten year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.10, took.ACT) %>%
  filter(grad.year.10 != "After 2023") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.10, var.col = data$took.ACT, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE,
         file = "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/took-ACT-10.html")
```
:::

```{r echo = FALSE}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.1, grad.year.5, grad.year.10, took.ACT) %>%
  write_csv(., "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/took-ACT.csv")

```

<br>

## `ACTCompositeScore`

### **Key Findings**

There is a statistically significant relationship between **ACT Composite Score** and post‑graduation outcomes in all three timeframes (ANOVA across outcome groups):

- **1 year after graduation**: F(4, 27079) = **387.39**, p \< .001
- **5 years after graduation**: F(4, 20617) = **159.11**, p \< .001
- **10 years after graduation**: F(3, 10486) = **119.50**, p \< .001

The results show a **clear and consistent pattern**: students with **higher ACT scores are more likely to attend postsecondary and later secure meaningful statewide employment**, while students with **lower ACT scores are more concentrated in regional meaningful jobs, not meaningful employment, or disconnected outcomes**.

### **Interpretation Over Time (Tukey HSD highlights)**

**Year 1:**

- **Postsecondary attendees** have ACT scores \~2.8 points higher than those in **regional meaningful employment** (p \< .001).
- **Not meaningful** (+0.88) and **No MN record** (+1.43) also score above **regional meaningful** (both p \< .001).
- Takeaway: **regional meaningful employment is tied to lower ACT scores**, while higher scores track into education and other categories.

**Year 5:**

- **Postsecondary** (+1.87), **statewide meaningful** (+1.84), and **No MN record** (+1.69) all score significantly above **regional meaningful** (p \< .001).
- **Not meaningful** is \~0.62 higher than regional meaningful (p \< .001).
- Takeaway: by five years, **regional meaningful employment continues to come from lower ACT backgrounds**, while higher scores predict broader education and statewide employment.

**Year 10:**

- **Statewide meaningful** (+1.64) and **No MN record** (+1.75) have higher ACT scores than **regional meaningful** (p \< .001).
- **Not meaningful** (+0.43) is also above **regional meaningful** (p = .005).
- Takeaway: a decade out, **regional meaningful employment is consistently tied to lower ACT scores**, while **higher scores are associated with statewide employment or exit from tracked MN employment**.

Overall, the ACT Composite Score reinforces a strong sorting effect: **higher scores steer students toward education and statewide jobs, while regional meaningful employment is more common among lower scorers**.

::: panel-tabset
### One year after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments ACTCompositeScore one year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.1, ACTCompositeScore) %>%
  filter(grad.year.1 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.1) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .25),
         q3 = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .75),
         iqr = IQR(ACTCompositeScore),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(ACTCompositeScore > low.outlier,
         ACTCompositeScore < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.1) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .25),
            Median = median(ACTCompositeScore),
            Q3 = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .75),
            Max = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, 1),
            Mean = mean(ACTCompositeScore)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.1, ACTCompositeScore, fill = grad.year.1, group = grad.year.1)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.1, y = 40, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of ACT composite score by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 45))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(ACTCompositeScore ~ grad.year.1, data = data)

summary

anova_df <- broom::tidy(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

tukey_df <- broom::tidy(TukeyHSD(summary))

wb <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb, "ANOVA")
addWorksheet(wb, "TukeyHSD")
writeData(wb, "ANOVA", anova_df)
writeData(wb, "TukeyHSD", tukey_df)
saveWorkbook(wb, "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/ACTCompositeScore-1.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)


```

### Five years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments ACTCompositeScore five year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.5, ACTCompositeScore) %>%
  filter(grad.year.5 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.5) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .25),
         q3 = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .75),
         iqr = IQR(ACTCompositeScore),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(ACTCompositeScore > low.outlier,
         ACTCompositeScore < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.5) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .25),
            Median = median(ACTCompositeScore),
            Q3 = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .75),
            Max = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, 1),
            Mean = mean(ACTCompositeScore)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.5, ACTCompositeScore, fill = grad.year.5, group = grad.year.5)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.5, y = 40, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of ACT composite score by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 45))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(ACTCompositeScore ~ grad.year.5, data = data)

summary

anova_df <- broom::tidy(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

tukey_df <- broom::tidy(TukeyHSD(summary))

wb <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb, "ANOVA")
addWorksheet(wb, "TukeyHSD")
writeData(wb, "ANOVA", anova_df)
writeData(wb, "TukeyHSD", tukey_df)
saveWorkbook(wb, "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/ACTCompositeScore-5.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)

```

### Ten years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments ACTCompositeScore ten year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.10, ACTCompositeScore) %>%
  filter(grad.year.10 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.10) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .25),
         q3 = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .75),
         iqr = IQR(ACTCompositeScore),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(ACTCompositeScore > low.outlier,
         ACTCompositeScore < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.10) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .25),
            Median = median(ACTCompositeScore),
            Q3 = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, .75),
            Max = quantile(ACTCompositeScore, 1),
            Mean = mean(ACTCompositeScore)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.10, ACTCompositeScore, fill = grad.year.10, group = grad.year.10)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.10, y = 40, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of ACT composite score by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 40))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(ACTCompositeScore ~ grad.year.10, data = data)

summary

anova_df <- broom::tidy(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

tukey_df <- broom::tidy(TukeyHSD(summary))

wb <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb, "ANOVA")
addWorksheet(wb, "TukeyHSD")
writeData(wb, "ANOVA", anova_df)
writeData(wb, "TukeyHSD", tukey_df)
saveWorkbook(wb, "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/ACTCompositeScore-10.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)

```
:::

```{r echo=FALSE}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.1, grad.year.5, grad.year.10, ACTCompositeScore) %>%
  write_csv(., "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/ACTCompositeScore.csv")

```

<br>

## `ap.exam`

### **Key Findings**

There is a statistically significant relationship between **AP exam participation** and post-graduation outcomes in all three timeframes:

- 1 year after graduation: χ²(4) = **822.44**, Cramer’s V = **0.133**, p \< .001, N = 46,777【581†source】
- 5 years after graduation: χ²(4) = **301.32**, Cramer’s V = **0.093**, p \< .001, N = 34,683【582†source】
- 10 years after graduation: χ²(3) = **113.60**, Cramer’s V = **0.076**, p \< .001, N = 19,828【583†source】

The strength of association is **strongest in the first year** and declines over time, though remains statistically significant. The primary relationship is that **AP exam participants are much more likely to attend postsecondary, while non‑participants are more likely to enter meaningful regional employment**.

### **Interpretation Over Time**

**1 Year After Graduation:** Regional meaningful employment is substantially higher among **non‑AP students (16.1% vs. 7.7%)**, while AP exam participants are much more likely to be in postsecondary (68.8% vs. 45.7%). Disconnection is lower for AP participants (6.7% vs. 12.2%)【581†source】.

**5 Years After Graduation:** Non‑AP students retain an advantage in regional meaningful employment (27.4% vs. 17.1%), while AP participants show higher rates of **statewide meaningful employment (19.8% vs. 14.7%)** and continued postsecondary enrollment (23.7% vs. 15.3%). Disconnection rates are similar (\~21%)【582†source】.

**10 Years After Graduation:** Regional meaningful employment remains higher for non‑AP students (29.9% vs. 22.7%). AP participants hold an advantage in **statewide meaningful employment (27.0% vs. 18.7%)**. Disconnection is slightly higher for AP participants (36.0% vs. 32.1%), suggesting some trade‑off in regional attachment over time【583†source】.

Overall, AP exam participation sorts students into pathways favoring **education and statewide employment**, while non‑participants maintain stronger ties to **regional meaningful employment**.

::: panel-tabset
### One year after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments ap.exam one year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.1, ap.exam) %>%
  filter(grad.year.1 != "After 2023") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.1, var.col = data$ap.exam, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE,
         file = "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/ap-exam-1.html")
```

### Five years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments ap.exam five year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.5, ap.exam) %>%
  filter(grad.year.5 != "After 2023") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.5, var.col = data$ap.exam, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE,
         file = "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/ap-exam-5.html")
```

### Ten years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments ap.exam ten year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.10, ap.exam) %>%
  filter(grad.year.10 != "After 2023") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.10, var.col = data$ap.exam, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE,
         file = "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/ap-exam-10.html")
```
:::

<br>

## `sat.taken`

### **Key Findings**

There is a statistically significant relationship between **taking the SAT** and post-graduation outcomes in all three timeframes:

- 1 year after graduation: χ²(4) = **228.74**, Cramer’s V = **0.070**, p \< .001, N = 46,777【595†source】
- 5 years after graduation: χ²(4) = **188.76**, Cramer’s V = **0.074**, p \< .001, N = 34,683【596†source】
- 10 years after graduation: χ²(3) = **99.99**, Cramer’s V = **0.071**, p \< .001, N = 19,828【597†source】

The effect sizes are **small but consistent**. The key pattern is that **SAT takers are far more likely to attend postsecondary education**, while **non‑takers are more likely to enter meaningful employment in the region**.

### **Interpretation Over Time**

**1 Year After Graduation:** SAT takers are overwhelmingly in postsecondary (73.5% vs. 47.3% for non‑takers). Regional meaningful employment is much lower for SAT takers (3.2% vs. 15.5%), while disconnection is also somewhat lower (9.0% vs. 11.7%)【595†source】.

**5 Years After Graduation:** SAT takers maintain higher postsecondary enrollment (30.1% vs. 15.8%). Regional meaningful employment is still much lower (7.6% vs. 26.8%). Interestingly, SAT takers are slightly more likely to be in meaningful employment statewide (18.2% vs. 15.1%). Disconnection is higher among SAT takers (28.6% vs. 20.9%)【596†source】.

**10 Years After Graduation:** SAT takers continue to show **very low regional meaningful employment (11.5% vs. 29.5%)**, while statewide meaningful employment is similar (18.6% vs. 19.4%). Disconnection is much higher among SAT takers (55.9% vs. 32.0%)【597†source】.

Overall, SAT participation identifies a small group of students who overwhelmingly pursue postsecondary initially, but long‑term outcomes show **weak regional attachment and much higher disconnection**, suggesting that **SAT takers may be more mobile, pursue opportunities outside the region or state, or leave tracked employment systems altogether**.

::: panel-tabset
### One year after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments sat.taken one year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.1, sat.taken) %>%
  filter(grad.year.1 != "After 2023") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.1, var.col = data$sat.taken, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE,
         file = "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/sat-taken-1.html")
```

### Five years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments sat.taken five year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.5, sat.taken) %>%
  filter(grad.year.5 != "After 2023") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.5, var.col = data$sat.taken, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE,
         file = "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/sat-taken-5.html")
```

### Ten years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments sat.taken ten year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.10, sat.taken) %>%
  filter(grad.year.10 != "After 2023") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.10, var.col = data$sat.taken, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE,
         file = "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/sat-taken-10.html")
```
:::

<br>

## `MCA.R`

### **Key Findings**

There is a statistically significant relationship between **MCA Reading scores** and post‑graduation outcomes in all three timeframes (ANOVA across outcome groups):

- **1 year after graduation**: F(4, 42500) = **689.45**, p \< .001
- **5 years after graduation**: F(4, 31013) = **119.45**, p \< .001
- **10 years after graduation**: F(3, 17590) = **237.15**, p \< .001

The results show a **very strong early effect** that remains significant over time. The primary pattern is that **higher MCA Reading scores are associated with postsecondary enrollment and statewide meaningful employment, while lower scores are more tied to regional meaningful employment or not meaningful/disconnected outcomes**.

**Interpretation Over Time (Tukey HSD highlights)**

**Year 1:**

- **Postsecondary attendees** score about **+0.26 higher** than regional meaningful workers (p \< .001).
- **Not meaningful** (+0.09) and **No MN record** (+0.14) also score higher than regional meaningful (p ≤ .02).
- Takeaway: **regional meaningful employment is linked to lower reading scores**, while higher scores track into postsecondary and other categories.

**Year 5:**

- **Postsecondary** (+0.21), **statewide meaningful** (+0.17), and **No MN record** (+0.08) all score above **regional meaningful** (p \< .001).
- **Not meaningful** scores slightly below regional meaningful (−0.17, p \< .001).
- Takeaway: by five years, **regional meaningful employment sits lower on the MCA-R spectrum**, while postsecondary and statewide jobs skew higher.

**Year 10:**

- **Statewide meaningful** (+0.18) and **No MN record** (+0.14) are significantly above **regional meaningful** (p \< .001).
- **Not meaningful** scores considerably lower (−0.25, p \< .001).
- Takeaway: a decade out, **regional meaningful employment remains tied to lower MCA-R scores**, with higher scores concentrated in statewide meaningful employment or outside tracked records.

Overall, MCA Reading reinforces that **academic achievement predicts longer-term educational and statewide work pathways**, while **lower scores are associated with stronger regional attachment but also greater risk of not meaningful outcomes**.

::: panel-tabset
### One year after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments MCA.R one year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.1, MCA.R) %>%
  filter(grad.year.1 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.1) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(MCA.R, .25),
         q3 = quantile(MCA.R, .75),
         iqr = IQR(MCA.R),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(MCA.R > low.outlier,
         MCA.R < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.1) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(MCA.R, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(MCA.R, .25),
            Median = median(MCA.R),
            Q3 = quantile(MCA.R, .75),
            Max = quantile(MCA.R, 1),
            Mean = mean(MCA.R)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.1, MCA.R, fill = grad.year.1, group = grad.year.1)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.1, y = 5, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of MCA-Reading score by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 6))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(MCA.R ~ grad.year.1, data = data)

summary

anova_df <- broom::tidy(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

tukey_df <- broom::tidy(TukeyHSD(summary))

wb <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb, "ANOVA")
addWorksheet(wb, "TukeyHSD")
writeData(wb, "ANOVA", anova_df)
writeData(wb, "TukeyHSD", tukey_df)
saveWorkbook(wb, "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/MCA-R-1.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)


```

### Five years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments MCA.R five year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.5, MCA.R) %>%
  filter(grad.year.5 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.5) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(MCA.R, .25),
         q3 = quantile(MCA.R, .75),
         iqr = IQR(MCA.R),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(MCA.R > low.outlier,
         MCA.R < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.5) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(MCA.R, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(MCA.R, .25),
            Median = median(MCA.R),
            Q3 = quantile(MCA.R, .75),
            Max = quantile(MCA.R, 1),
            Mean = mean(MCA.R)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.5, MCA.R, fill = grad.year.5, group = grad.year.5)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.5, y = 5, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of MCA-Reading score by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 6))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(MCA.R ~ grad.year.5, data = data)

summary

anova_df <- broom::tidy(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

tukey_df <- broom::tidy(TukeyHSD(summary))

wb <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb, "ANOVA")
addWorksheet(wb, "TukeyHSD")
writeData(wb, "ANOVA", anova_df)
writeData(wb, "TukeyHSD", tukey_df)
saveWorkbook(wb, "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/MCA-R-5.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)

```

### Ten years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments MCA.R ten year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.10, MCA.R) %>%
  filter(grad.year.10 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.10) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(MCA.R, .25),
         q3 = quantile(MCA.R, .75),
         iqr = IQR(MCA.R),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(MCA.R > low.outlier,
         MCA.R < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.10) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(MCA.R, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(MCA.R, .25),
            Median = median(MCA.R),
            Q3 = quantile(MCA.R, .75),
            Max = quantile(MCA.R, 1),
            Mean = mean(MCA.R)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.10, MCA.R, fill = grad.year.10, group = grad.year.10)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.10, y = 5, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of MCA-Reading score by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 6))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(MCA.R ~ grad.year.10, data = data)

summary

anova_df <- broom::tidy(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

tukey_df <- broom::tidy(TukeyHSD(summary))

wb <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb, "ANOVA")
addWorksheet(wb, "TukeyHSD")
writeData(wb, "ANOVA", anova_df)
writeData(wb, "TukeyHSD", tukey_df)
saveWorkbook(wb, "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/MCA-R-10.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)

```
:::

<br>

## `MCA.S`

### **Key Findings**

There is a statistically significant relationship between **MCA Science scores** and post‑graduation outcomes in all three timeframes (ANOVA across outcome groups):

- **1 year after graduation**: F(4, 37501) = **544.02**, p \< .001
- **5 years after graduation**: F(4, 26050) = **160.29**, p \< .001
- **10 years after graduation**: F(3, 11879) = **81.74**, p \< .001

The results show a **very strong early effect** that remains significant through 10 years. The key pattern is that **higher MCA Science scores are associated with postsecondary education and statewide meaningful employment, while lower scores are tied to regional meaningful employment or not meaningful outcomes**.

**Interpretation Over Time (Tukey HSD highlights)**

**Year 1:**

- Postsecondary attendees score substantially higher than **regional meaningful** (+0.38, p \< .001).
- **Statewide meaningful** (+0.07) also scores higher than regional meaningful (p = .025).
- **Not meaningful** (−0.07) and **No MN record** (−0.12) score below **postsecondary**.
- Takeaway: early on, **regional meaningful work is tied to lower MCA-S scores**, while **education and statewide jobs are tied to higher scores**.

**Year 5:**

- **Statewide meaningful (+0.25)** and **postsecondary (+0.32)** are far above **regional meaningful** (p \< .001).
- **No MN record (+0.11)** is also higher.
- **Not meaningful (−0.04)** is slightly below regional meaningful (p = .044).
- Takeaway: by 5 years, the **gap widens**, with statewide and postsecondary pathways clearly tied to higher MCA-S scores, while regional meaningful remains lower.

**Year 10:**

- **Statewide meaningful (+0.27)** and **No MN record (+0.18)** score above **regional meaningful** (p \< .001).
- **Not meaningful (−0.31)** scores well below regional meaningful (p \< .001).
- Takeaway: a decade out, **regional meaningful employment remains linked to lower MCA-S scores**, while higher scores push students into statewide jobs or out of tracked MN employment.

Overall, MCA Science mirrors the MCA Reading pattern: **academic achievement is strongly tied to educational and statewide pathways, while lower achievement is associated with stronger regional attachment or risk of weaker employment outcomes**.

::: panel-tabset
### One year after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments MCA.S one year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.1, MCA.S) %>%
  filter(grad.year.1 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.1) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(MCA.S, .25),
         q3 = quantile(MCA.S, .75),
         iqr = IQR(MCA.S),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(MCA.S > low.outlier,
         MCA.S < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.1) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(MCA.S, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(MCA.S, .25),
            Median = median(MCA.S),
            Q3 = quantile(MCA.S, .75),
            Max = quantile(MCA.S, 1),
            Mean = mean(MCA.S)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.1, MCA.S, fill = grad.year.1, group = grad.year.1)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.1, y = 5, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of MCA-Science score by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 6))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(MCA.S ~ grad.year.1, data = data)

summary

anova_df <- broom::tidy(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

tukey_df <- broom::tidy(TukeyHSD(summary))

wb <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb, "ANOVA")
addWorksheet(wb, "TukeyHSD")
writeData(wb, "ANOVA", anova_df)
writeData(wb, "TukeyHSD", tukey_df)
saveWorkbook(wb, "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/MCA-S-1.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)

```

### Five years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments MCA.S five year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.5, MCA.S) %>%
  filter(grad.year.5 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.5) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(MCA.S, .25),
         q3 = quantile(MCA.S, .75),
         iqr = IQR(MCA.S),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(MCA.S > low.outlier,
         MCA.S < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.5) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(MCA.S, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(MCA.S, .25),
            Median = median(MCA.S),
            Q3 = quantile(MCA.S, .75),
            Max = quantile(MCA.S, 1),
            Mean = mean(MCA.S)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.5, MCA.S, fill = grad.year.5, group = grad.year.5)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.5, y = 5, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of MCA-Science score by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 6))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(MCA.S ~ grad.year.5, data = data)

summary

anova_df <- broom::tidy(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

tukey_df <- broom::tidy(TukeyHSD(summary))

wb <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb, "ANOVA")
addWorksheet(wb, "TukeyHSD")
writeData(wb, "ANOVA", anova_df)
writeData(wb, "TukeyHSD", tukey_df)
saveWorkbook(wb, "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/MCA-S-5.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)
```

### Ten years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments MCA.S ten year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.10, MCA.S) %>%
  filter(grad.year.10 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.10) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(MCA.S, .25),
         q3 = quantile(MCA.S, .75),
         iqr = IQR(MCA.S),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(MCA.S > low.outlier,
         MCA.S < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.10) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(MCA.S, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(MCA.S, .25),
            Median = median(MCA.S),
            Q3 = quantile(MCA.S, .75),
            Max = quantile(MCA.S, 1),
            Mean = mean(MCA.S)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.10, MCA.S, fill = grad.year.10, group = grad.year.10)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.10, y = 5, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of MCA-Science score by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 6))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(MCA.S ~ grad.year.10, data = data)

summary

anova_df <- broom::tidy(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

tukey_df <- broom::tidy(TukeyHSD(summary))

wb <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb, "ANOVA")
addWorksheet(wb, "TukeyHSD")
writeData(wb, "ANOVA", anova_df)
writeData(wb, "TukeyHSD", tukey_df)
saveWorkbook(wb, "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/MCA-S-10.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)

```
:::

<br>

## `MCA.M`

### **Key Findings**

There is a statistically significant relationship between **MCA Math scores** and post‑graduation outcomes in all three timeframes (ANOVA across outcome groups):

- **1 year after graduation**: F(4, 42550) = **1344.53**, p \< .001
- **5 years after graduation**: F(4, 33107) = **152.39**, p \< .001
- **10 years after graduation**: F(3, 18728) = **94.98**, p \< .001

The results show an **especially strong relationship in the first year**, with effects tapering but still significant through Year 10. The key pattern is that **higher MCA Math scores are linked to postsecondary enrollment and statewide meaningful employment, while lower scores are tied to regional meaningful employment and weaker outcomes**.

**Interpretation Over Time (Tukey HSD highlights)**

**Year 1:**

- **Postsecondary attendees** score much higher than **regional meaningful** (+0.58, p \< .001).
- **Statewide meaningful** is also above **regional meaningful** (+0.07, p = .026).
- **Not meaningful (−0.06)** and **No MN record (−0.17)** score lower than **regional meaningful** (p ≤ .001).
- Takeaway: early on, **regional meaningful employment is positioned between low‑scoring disconnected outcomes and high‑scoring education pathways**.

**Year 5:**

- **Postsecondary (+0.28)** and **statewide meaningful (+0.27)** both score significantly above **regional meaningful** (p \< .001).
- **Not meaningful (−0.08)** and **No MN record (−0.24)** score below regional meaningful (p \< .001).
- Takeaway: by five years, **regional meaningful work is associated with lower scores relative to statewide and postsecondary**, but still stronger than disconnected categories.

**Year 10:**

- **Statewide meaningful** (+0.29) remains higher than **regional meaningful** (p \< .001).
- **No MN record** shows a mixed pattern: one contrast indicates higher scores (+0.13, p \< .001) while another shows lower scores (−0.15, p \< .001), reflecting sub‑patterns in this group.
- **Not meaningful (−0.10 to −0.38)** scores consistently lower than regional meaningful (p \< .001).
- Takeaway: a decade out, **regional meaningful jobs remain linked to mid‑range math scores**, lower than statewide/postsecondary but higher than disconnected outcomes.

Overall, MCA Math reinforces the trend seen in other MCA measures: **higher achievement channels students into education and statewide employment, while regional meaningful employment is associated with lower but not the lowest scores, positioning it as a middle pathway**.

::: panel-tabset
### One year after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments MCA.M one year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.1, MCA.M) %>%
  filter(grad.year.1 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.1) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(MCA.M, .25),
         q3 = quantile(MCA.M, .75),
         iqr = IQR(MCA.M),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(MCA.M > low.outlier,
         MCA.M < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.1) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(MCA.M, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(MCA.M, .25),
            Median = median(MCA.M),
            Q3 = quantile(MCA.M, .75),
            Max = quantile(MCA.M, 1),
            Mean = mean(MCA.M)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.1, MCA.M, fill = grad.year.1, group = grad.year.1)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.1, y = 5, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of MCA-Math score by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 6))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(MCA.M ~ grad.year.1, data = data)

summary

anova_df <- broom::tidy(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

tukey_df <- broom::tidy(TukeyHSD(summary))

wb <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb, "ANOVA")
addWorksheet(wb, "TukeyHSD")
writeData(wb, "ANOVA", anova_df)
writeData(wb, "TukeyHSD", tukey_df)
saveWorkbook(wb, "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/MCA-M-1.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)

```

### Five years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments MCA.M five year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.5, MCA.M) %>%
  filter(grad.year.5 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.5) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(MCA.M, .25),
         q3 = quantile(MCA.M, .75),
         iqr = IQR(MCA.M),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(MCA.M > low.outlier,
         MCA.M < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.5) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(MCA.M, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(MCA.M, .25),
            Median = median(MCA.M),
            Q3 = quantile(MCA.M, .75),
            Max = quantile(MCA.M, 1),
            Mean = mean(MCA.M)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.5, MCA.M, fill = grad.year.5, group = grad.year.5)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.5, y = 5, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of MCA-Math score by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 6))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(MCA.M ~ grad.year.5, data = data)

summary

anova_df <- broom::tidy(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

tukey_df <- broom::tidy(TukeyHSD(summary))

wb <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb, "ANOVA")
addWorksheet(wb, "TukeyHSD")
writeData(wb, "ANOVA", anova_df)
writeData(wb, "TukeyHSD", tukey_df)
saveWorkbook(wb, "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/MCA-M-5.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)

```

### Ten years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments MCA.M ten year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.10, MCA.M) %>%
  filter(grad.year.10 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.10) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(MCA.M, .25),
         q3 = quantile(MCA.M, .75),
         iqr = IQR(MCA.M),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(MCA.M > low.outlier,
         MCA.M < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.10) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(MCA.M, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(MCA.M, .25),
            Median = median(MCA.M),
            Q3 = quantile(MCA.M, .75),
            Max = quantile(MCA.M, 1),
            Mean = mean(MCA.M)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.10, MCA.M, fill = grad.year.10, group = grad.year.10)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.10, y = 5, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of MCA-Math score by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 6))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(MCA.M ~ grad.year.10, data = data)

summary

anova_df <- broom::tidy(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

tukey_df <- broom::tidy(TukeyHSD(summary))

wb <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb, "ANOVA")
addWorksheet(wb, "TukeyHSD")
writeData(wb, "ANOVA", anova_df)
writeData(wb, "TukeyHSD", tukey_df)
saveWorkbook(wb, "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/MCA-M-10.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)

```
:::

<br>

## `cte.achievement`

### **Key Findings**

There is a statistically significant relationship between **CTE achievement** (Concentrator/Completer, Participant, No CTE) and post‑graduation outcomes across all three timeframes:

- **1 year after graduation**: χ²(8) = **508.14**, Cramer’s V = **0.074**, p \< .001, N = 46,777【663†source】
- **5 years after graduation**: χ²(8) = **307.50**, Cramer’s V = **0.067**, p \< .001, N = 34,683【664†source】
- **10 years after graduation**: χ²(6) = **174.53**, Cramer’s V = **0.066**, p \< .001, N = 19,828【665†source】

The associations are modest but consistent. The clearest relationship is that **CTE Concentrators/Completers are more likely to enter meaningful employment in the region**, while students with no CTE are more likely to be in postsecondary or outside regional attachment.

### **Interpretation Over Time**

**1 Year After Graduation:** Regional meaningful employment is highest for **CTE Concentrators/Completers (19.4%)**, compared to **Participants (15.1%)** and **No CTE students (12.6%)**. Postsecondary enrollment is much higher among **No CTE (51.3%)** and **Participants (49.2%)** than Concentrators/Completers (41.5%). This suggests that **CTE pathways steer students more quickly into regional work** while non‑CTE students delay employment through postsecondary【663†source】.

**5 Years After Graduation:** The regional employment gap persists: **31.9% for Concentrators/Completers vs. 27.2% for Participants and 22.9% for No CTE**. Postsecondary attendance is relatively low across all groups (13–17%), but still higher for those without CTE. Concentrators/Completers maintain stronger local employment connections, while non‑CTE students lean slightly more toward statewide employment (15.7%)【664†source】.

**10 Years After Graduation:** Regional meaningful employment remains strongest among **Concentrators/Completers (34.6%)**, compared to **Participants (29.1%)** and **No CTE students (25.5%)**. Statewide meaningful employment is more evenly distributed, while disconnection is highest among those with **No CTE (35.5%)**. The long‑term pattern confirms that **CTE achievement reinforces regional employment pathways**, while students outside CTE are more likely to leave the region or experience disconnection【665†source】.

Overall, CTE achievement consistently predicts **stronger regional workforce attachment**, distinguishing Concentrators/Completers as the group most likely to remain engaged in meaningful employment within the region over time.

::: panel-tabset
### One year after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments cte.achievement one year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.1, cte.achievement) %>%
  filter(grad.year.1 != "After 2023") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.1, var.col = data$cte.achievement, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE,
         file = "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/cte-achievement-1.html")
```

### Five years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments cte.achievement five year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.5, cte.achievement) %>%
  filter(grad.year.5 != "After 2023") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.5, var.col = data$cte.achievement, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE,
         file = "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/cte-achievement-5.html")
```

### Ten years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments cte.achievement ten year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.10, cte.achievement) %>%
  filter(grad.year.10 != "After 2023") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.10, var.col = data$cte.achievement, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE,
         file = "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/cte-achievement-10.html")
```
:::

<br>

## `total.cte.courses.taken`

### **Key Findings**

There is a statistically significant relationship between the **number of CTE courses taken** and post‑graduation outcomes in all three timeframes (ANOVA across outcome groups):

- **1 year after graduation**: F(4, 45696) = **232.40**, p \< .001
- **5 years after graduation**: F(4, 33828) = **104.15**, p \< .001
- **10 years after graduation**: F(3, 19320) = **36.17**, p \< .001

The results show a consistent pattern: **students who took more CTE courses are more likely to enter meaningful regional employment**, while students with fewer CTE courses lean toward postsecondary, statewide, or disconnected pathways.

### **Interpretation Over Time (Tukey HSD highlights)**

**Year 1:**

- Regional meaningful employment is associated with **higher CTE course counts** compared to postsecondary (−0.72, p \< .001), not meaningful (−0.21, p \< .001), and no MN record (−0.37, p \< .001).
- Postsecondary students have the lowest CTE course counts, confirming that **heavier CTE load ties directly into early regional work**.

**Year 5:**

- Regional meaningful again shows **higher CTE courses** than postsecondary (−0.61, p \< .001), meaningful MN (−0.44, p \< .001), not meaningful (−0.33, p \< .001), and no MN record (−0.45, p \< .001).
- Postsecondary and statewide workers come from lower CTE course backgrounds.
- Takeaway: at five years, **regional meaningful work is strongly linked to CTE engagement**, while those with fewer CTE courses are dispersed across statewide or disconnected outcomes.

**Year 10:**

- Regional meaningful still has higher CTE courses compared to statewide meaningful (−0.31, p \< .001) and no MN record (−0.31, p \< .001).
- Not meaningful outcomes also show slightly higher CTE courses than regional (+0.21, p \< .001), reflecting a mixed subgroup.
- Takeaway: a decade out, **regional meaningful employment is still concentrated among heavier CTE participants**, reinforcing CTE as a strong predictor of regional attachment.

Overall, total CTE course load demonstrates that **increased CTE participation anchors students more firmly in regional employment**, while lighter CTE engagement is linked to education and broader employment pathways.

::: panel-tabset
### One year after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments total.cte.courses.taken one year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.1, total.cte.courses.taken) %>%
  filter(grad.year.1 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.1) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(total.cte.courses.taken, .25),
         q3 = quantile(total.cte.courses.taken, .75),
         iqr = IQR(total.cte.courses.taken),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(total.cte.courses.taken > low.outlier,
         total.cte.courses.taken < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.1) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(total.cte.courses.taken, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(total.cte.courses.taken, .25),
            Median = median(total.cte.courses.taken),
            Q3 = quantile(total.cte.courses.taken, .75),
            Max = quantile(total.cte.courses.taken, 1),
            Mean = mean(total.cte.courses.taken)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.1, total.cte.courses.taken, fill = grad.year.1, group = grad.year.1)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.1, y = 5, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of number of CTE courses by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 6))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(total.cte.courses.taken ~ grad.year.1, data = data)

summary

anova_df <- broom::tidy(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

tukey_df <- broom::tidy(TukeyHSD(summary))

wb <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb, "ANOVA")
addWorksheet(wb, "TukeyHSD")
writeData(wb, "ANOVA", anova_df)
writeData(wb, "TukeyHSD", tukey_df)
saveWorkbook(wb, "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/total-cte-courses-taken-1.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)


```

### Five years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments total.cte.courses.taken five year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.5, total.cte.courses.taken) %>%
  filter(grad.year.5 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.5) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(total.cte.courses.taken, .25),
         q3 = quantile(total.cte.courses.taken, .75),
         iqr = IQR(total.cte.courses.taken),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(total.cte.courses.taken > low.outlier,
         total.cte.courses.taken < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.5) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(total.cte.courses.taken, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(total.cte.courses.taken, .25),
            Median = median(total.cte.courses.taken),
            Q3 = quantile(total.cte.courses.taken, .75),
            Max = quantile(total.cte.courses.taken, 1),
            Mean = mean(total.cte.courses.taken)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.5, total.cte.courses.taken, fill = grad.year.5, group = grad.year.5)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.5, y = 5, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of number of CTE courses by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 6))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(total.cte.courses.taken ~ grad.year.5, data = data)

summary

anova_df <- broom::tidy(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

tukey_df <- broom::tidy(TukeyHSD(summary))

wb <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb, "ANOVA")
addWorksheet(wb, "TukeyHSD")
writeData(wb, "ANOVA", anova_df)
writeData(wb, "TukeyHSD", tukey_df)
saveWorkbook(wb, "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/total-cte-courses-taken-5.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)
```

### Ten years after graduating

```{r hs accomplishments total.cte.courses.taken ten year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.10, total.cte.courses.taken) %>%
  filter(grad.year.10 != "After 2023") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  group_by(grad.year.10) %>%
  mutate(q1 = quantile(total.cte.courses.taken, .25),
         q3 = quantile(total.cte.courses.taken, .75),
         iqr = IQR(total.cte.courses.taken),
         low.outlier = q1 - 1.5*iqr,
         high.outlier = q3 + 1.5*iqr) %>%
  filter(total.cte.courses.taken > low.outlier,
         total.cte.courses.taken < high.outlier) %>%
  ungroup()

labels <- data %>%
  group_by(grad.year.10) %>%
  summarize(Min = quantile(total.cte.courses.taken, .0),
            Q1 = quantile(total.cte.courses.taken, .25),
            Median = median(total.cte.courses.taken),
            Q3 = quantile(total.cte.courses.taken, .75),
            Max = quantile(total.cte.courses.taken, 1),
            Mean = mean(total.cte.courses.taken)) %>%
  ungroup()

plot <- ggplot(data, aes(grad.year.10, total.cte.courses.taken, fill = grad.year.10, group = grad.year.10)) +
  geom_boxplot_interactive() +
  geom_label(data = labels, aes(x = grad.year.10, y = 5, label = paste("Min: ", comma(Min, accuracy = .1), "\nQ1: ", comma(Q1, accuracy = .1), "\nMedian: ", comma(Median, accuracy = .1), "\nQ3: ", comma(Q3, accuracy = .1), "\nMax: ", comma(Max, accuracy = ), "\nMean: ", comma(Mean, accuracy = .1))), color = "white") +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", title = "Boxplots of number of CTE courses by employment state")+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0, 6))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = brewer.pal(n = 6, "RdYlBu")) +
  theme_bar+
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        text = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = .9))


girafe(ggobj = plot, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 10) %>%
  girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                 opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE)) 


summary <- aov(total.cte.courses.taken ~ grad.year.10, data = data)

summary

anova_df <- broom::tidy(summary)

TukeyHSD(summary)

tukey_df <- broom::tidy(TukeyHSD(summary))

wb <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb, "ANOVA")
addWorksheet(wb, "TukeyHSD")
writeData(wb, "ANOVA", anova_df)
writeData(wb, "TukeyHSD", tukey_df)
saveWorkbook(wb, "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/High school accomplishments/total-cte-courses-taken-10.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)

```
:::

<br>

# Post-secondary

We will analyze the following variables;

- `attended.ps.within.first.year.hsgrad`
- `attended.ps`
- `ps.grad`
- `ps.grad.InstitutionSector`
- `highest.cred.level`
- `ps.grad.location`

<br>

## Postsecondary Experience Section Summary

### Comparative Table of Effect Sizes Across Variables

| Variable | Year 1 | Year 5 | Year 10 | Peak Strength | Pattern of Change |
|------------|------------|------------|------------|------------|------------|
| Attended PS within 1st year | V = 0.542 | V = 0.246 | V = 0.101 | Year 1 | Extremely strong early effect, weakens but persists |
| Ever Attended PS | V = 0.683 | V = 0.342 | V = 0.137 | Year 1 | Very strong early, declines steadily |
| Postsecondary Graduation | V = 0.600 | V = 0.378 | V = 0.155 | Year 1 | Strong predictor, declines but remains significant |
| PS Grad by Institution Sector | V = 0.138 | V = 0.152 | V = 0.160 | Year 10 | Moderate, stable over time |
| Highest Credential Level | V = 0.366 | V = 0.231 | V = 0.132 | Year 1 | Strong early association, gradually weakens |
| PS Graduation Location | V = 0.197 | V = 0.207 | V = 0.164 | Year 5 | Moderate and consistent, strongest at year 5 |

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Key Patterns & Comparative Insights

**Strongest Associations**

- The **attendance variables** (attended within 1st year, ever attended) and **postsecondary graduation** have the **strongest early associations**, with Cramer’s V values above 0.5–0.6 at Year 1. These measures clearly separate students who pursue higher education from those who do not, with PS attenders/graduates far less likely to enter immediate regional work but more likely to gain stable employment outcomes over time.
- **Highest credential level** also shows strong early differentiation, with higher credentials linked to delayed workforce entry and eventual statewide mobility, while lower or mid‑level credentials tie more closely to regional jobs.

**Moderate Associations**

- **PS graduation location** and **institution sector** matter less than attendance or graduation itself but still provide meaningful distinctions. **Regional PS graduates** are far more likely to remain in local employment, while **graduates from 4‑year universities or from outside the region** show greater statewide mobility.

**Regional Employment Predictors**

- Non‑attenders and non‑graduates are consistently more likely to enter **regional meaningful employment** early, but they also carry much higher risks of disconnection in the long run.
- **CTE‑like pathways within postsecondary (2‑year institutions, Associate degrees, regional institutions)** show the strongest alignment with long‑term regional workforce attachment.
- **Bachelor’s+ degrees, 4‑year universities, and graduation outside MN** are consistently tied to statewide employment and mobility, reducing regional anchoring.

**Overall Insight**\
Postsecondary experiences draw a clear line between **regional anchoring vs. statewide mobility**.

1.  **Access and completion of postsecondary education** lead to broader statewide opportunities and reduced risk of disconnection.
2.  **Absence of postsecondary pathways** results in earlier regional employment but higher long‑term vulnerability.
3.  **The type, level, and location of postsecondary attainment** nuance this divide: regional and 2‑year pathways strengthen local attachment, while higher‑level and out‑of‑region pathways expand opportunities but reduce regional ties.

<br>

## `attended.ps.within.first.year.hsgrad`

### **Key Findings**

There is a statistically significant relationship between **attending postsecondary within the first year after high school** and post‑graduation outcomes across all timeframes:

- **1 year after graduation**: χ²(8) = **27,442.69**, Cramer’s V = **0.542**, p \< .001, N = 46,777【712†source】
- **5 years after graduation**: χ²(8) = **4,207.23**, Cramer’s V = **0.246**, p \< .001, N = 34,683【713†source】
- **10 years after graduation**: χ²(6) = **408.26**, Cramer’s V = **0.101**, p \< .001, N = 19,828【714†source】

The effect is **extremely strong in the first year** and weakens over time, though it remains statistically significant. The main pattern is that **students who attend PS within the first year are much less likely to be in regional meaningful employment, while those who do not attend are far more likely to start working in the region**.

### **Interpretation Over Time**

**Year 1:** Students who did not attend PS in the first year had a much higher rate of **regional meaningful employment (19.9%)** compared to attenders (12.8%). Conversely, attenders overwhelmingly show up as **still in postsecondary (74.6% vs. \~0%)**. Non‑attenders also show very high rates of disconnection (44.2% “not meaningful” and 30.1% “no MN record”). This reflects a clear divide: **attenders delay employment through school, while non‑attenders move directly into local jobs or into disconnected outcomes**【712†source】.

**Year 5:** Regional meaningful employment is somewhat higher among attenders (27.9%) than non‑attenders (23.9%). However, **statewide meaningful employment favors attenders (17.0% vs. 11.7%)**. Disconnection remains higher for non‑attenders (27.4% “not meaningful” vs. 18.8% among attenders; 37.0% “no MN record” vs. 14.8%). Thus by five years, **attenders are more successful statewide, while non‑attenders show weaker stability and higher disconnection**【713†source】.

**Year 10:** Regional meaningful employment continues to be higher for attenders (31.6%) compared to non‑attenders (23.0%). Attenders also remain stronger in statewide meaningful employment (21.6% vs. 14.4%). By contrast, non‑attenders face higher disconnection (41.7% “no MN record” vs. 28.9% for attenders). The long‑term pattern confirms that **early PS attendance reduces immediate regional attachment but supports statewide employment and better outcomes over time**, while **non‑attenders anchor more in the region early but face greater disconnection risks long‑term**【714†source】.

::: panel-tabset
### One year after graduating

```{r post-secondary attended.ps.within.first.year.hsgrad one year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.1, attended.ps.within.first.year.hsgrad) %>%
  filter(grad.year.1 != "After 2023") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.1, var.col = data$attended.ps.within.first.year.hsgrad, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE,
         file = "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/Post-secondary/attended-ps-within-first-year-hsgrad-1.html")
```

### Five years after graduating

```{r post-secondary attended.ps.within.first.year.hsgrad five year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.5, attended.ps.within.first.year.hsgrad) %>%
  filter(grad.year.5 != "After 2023") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.5, var.col = data$attended.ps.within.first.year.hsgrad, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE,
         file = "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/Post-secondary/attended-ps-within-first-year-hsgrad-5.html")
```

### Ten years after graduating

```{r post-secondary attended.ps.within.first.year.hsgrad ten year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.10, attended.ps.within.first.year.hsgrad) %>%
  filter(grad.year.10 != "After 2023") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.10, var.col = data$attended.ps.within.first.year.hsgrad, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE,
         file = "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/Post-secondary/attended-ps-within-first-year-hsgrad-10.html")
```
:::

<br>

## `attended.ps`

### **Key Findings**

There is a statistically significant relationship between **ever attending postsecondary** and post‑graduation outcomes across all timeframes:

- **1 year after graduation**: χ²(4) = **21,822.13**, Cramer’s V = **0.683**, p \< .001, N = 46,777【724†source】
- **5 years after graduation**: χ²(4) = **4,066.08**, Cramer’s V = **0.342**, p \< .001, N = 34,683【725†source】
- **10 years after graduation**: χ²(3) = **372.88**, Cramer’s V = **0.137**, p \< .001, N = 19,828【726†source】

The effect is **extremely strong in the first year** but declines steadily over time. The central pattern is that **students who attended postsecondary are far less likely to be in regional meaningful employment early on, though they become more evenly distributed between regional and statewide employment later**.

### **Interpretation Over Time**

**Year 1:** Students who did not attend PS show much higher **regional meaningful employment (19.9% vs. 13.3%)**. However, their outcomes also include far more disconnection: **44.2% “not meaningful” and 30.1% “no MN record”** compared to 10.5% and 3.5% among attenders. By contrast, attenders are overwhelmingly in **postsecondary (69.1%)**. This reflects a sharp early divide: **non‑attenders attach to local jobs or disconnect, while attenders delay employment for education**【724†source】.

**Year 5:** Regional meaningful employment becomes slightly higher for attenders (27.5% vs. 23.9%), and **statewide meaningful employment is also stronger among attenders (16.5% vs. 11.7%)**. Meanwhile, disconnection is far more common among non‑attenders (27.4% “not meaningful” and 37.0% “no record”) compared to 19% and 15% for attenders. Thus, by five years, **attenders gain more stable and broader employment outcomes**, while non‑attenders remain more vulnerable【725†source】.

**Year 10:** Regional meaningful employment favors attenders (31.4% vs. 23.0%), and their advantage in **statewide meaningful employment (21.1% vs. 14.4%)** continues. Non‑attenders remain much more disconnected (41.7% “no record” vs. 29.2% among attenders). Long‑term, this confirms that **attending postsecondary—even if not immediately—enhances both regional and statewide employment outcomes, while non‑attenders remain more likely to disconnect**【726†source】.

Overall, the evidence shows that **postsecondary attendance strongly reduces early local workforce entry but leads to more stable and broader employment later**, whereas non‑attenders enter regional work early but remain at higher risk of disconnection.

::: panel-tabset
### One year after graduating

```{r post-secondary attended.ps one year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.1, attended.ps) %>%
  filter(grad.year.1 != "After 2023") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.1, var.col = data$attended.ps, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE,
         file = "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/Post-secondary/attended-ps-1.html")
```

### Five years after graduating

```{r post-secondary attended.ps five year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.5, attended.ps) %>%
  filter(grad.year.5 != "After 2023") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.5, var.col = data$attended.ps, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE,
         file = "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/Post-secondary/attended-ps-5.html")
```

### Ten years after graduating

```{r post-secondary attended.ps ten year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.10, attended.ps) %>%
  filter(grad.year.10 != "After 2023") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.10, var.col = data$attended.ps, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE,
         file = "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/Post-secondary/attended-ps-10.html")
```
:::

<br>

## `ps.grad`

### **Key Findings**

There is a statistically significant relationship between **postsecondary graduation** and post‑graduation outcomes across all timeframes:

- **1 year after graduation**: χ²(4) = **15,285.21**, Cramer’s V = **0.600**, p \< .001, N = 42,459【736†source】
- **5 years after graduation**: χ²(4) = **4,882.22**, Cramer’s V = **0.378**, p \< .001, N = 34,144【737†source】
- **10 years after graduation**: χ²(3) = **476.15**, Cramer’s V = **0.155**, p \< .001, N = 19,828【738†source】

The effect is **very strong in the first year**, moderate by year 5, and still significant though weaker at year 10. The pattern shows that **graduates are initially concentrated in continued postsecondary enrollment**, but over time they transition into **statewide meaningful employment** and maintain somewhat stronger outcomes than non‑graduates.

### **Interpretation Over Time**

**Year 1:** Postsecondary graduates are overwhelmingly recorded as **still attending PS (77.4%)**, compared to only 19.4% of non‑graduates. Regional meaningful employment is lower for graduates (11.6% vs. 19.2%), while non‑graduates are far more likely to fall into **not meaningful (35%)** or **no MN record (20.8%)**. This shows the early divide: **graduates are embedded in education, while non‑graduates more often enter local work or disconnection**【736†source】.

**Year 5:** By this point, regional meaningful employment is similar between the two groups (26.9% graduates vs. 26.3% non‑graduates). However, **graduates are more likely to achieve statewide meaningful employment (18.0% vs. 12.6%)** and remain more represented in ongoing postsecondary enrollment (27.4% vs. 4.2%). Non‑graduates are much more disconnected, with 27.3% in “not meaningful” and 29.6% with no MN record【737†source】.

**Year 10:** Regional meaningful employment slightly favors graduates (33.0% vs. 25.6%). Graduates also maintain a strong advantage in statewide meaningful employment (23.4% vs. 15.6%). By contrast, non‑graduates are far more disconnected (36.7% “no MN record” vs. 28.0% for graduates). Long‑term, this confirms that **postsecondary graduation enhances both regional and statewide employment outcomes**, while **non‑graduates are more likely to face disconnection**【738†source】.

Overall, postsecondary graduation is a **critical predictor of long‑term stability**, reducing disconnection and improving both regional and statewide employment, while non‑graduates remain more vulnerable.

::: panel-tabset
### One year after graduating

```{r post-secondary ps.grad one year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.1, ps.grad) %>%
  filter(grad.year.1 != "After 2023") %>%
  filter(ps.grad != "Attending ps") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.1, var.col = data$ps.grad, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE,
         file = "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/Post-secondary/ps-grad-1.html")
```

### Five years after graduating

```{r post-secondary ps.grad five year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.5, ps.grad) %>%
  filter(grad.year.5 != "After 2023") %>%
  filter(ps.grad != "Attending ps") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.5, var.col = data$ps.grad, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE,
         file = "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/Post-secondary/ps-grad-5.html")
```

### Ten years after graduating

```{r post-secondary ps.grad ten year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.10, ps.grad) %>%
  filter(grad.year.10 != "After 2023") %>%
  filter(ps.grad != "Attending ps") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.10, var.col = data$ps.grad, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE,
         file = "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/Post-secondary/ps-grad-10.html")
```
:::

<br>

## `ps.grad.InstitutionSector`

### **Key Findings**

There is a statistically significant relationship between the **sector of postsecondary graduation** (4‑year public, 2‑year public, other) and post‑graduation outcomes across all timeframes:

- **1 year after graduation**: χ²(12) = **1313.46**, Cramer’s V = **0.138**, p \< .001, N = 22,962【748†source】
- **5 years after graduation**: χ²(12) = **1181.64**, Cramer’s V = **0.152**, p \< .001, N = 17,154【749†source】
- **10 years after graduation**: χ²(6) = **498.68**, Cramer’s V = **0.160**, p \< .001, N = 9,691【750†source】

The relationship is **moderate but consistent**, strengthening slightly by year 10. The main divide is between **4‑year and 2‑year graduates**, with different propensities toward statewide vs. regional employment.

### **Interpretation Over Time**

**Year 1:** Almost all graduates remain in postsecondary, but small differences emerge. **2‑year graduates show the highest early regional employment (20.6%)**, compared to 7.1% among “other” graduates and just 4.5% among 4‑year graduates. Graduates of 4‑year and “other” institutions overwhelmingly remain enrolled (86%+), delaying workforce entry【748†source】.

**Year 5:** Clearer differences appear. **2‑year graduates lead in regional meaningful employment (40.7%)**, while 4‑year graduates show higher statewide employment (21.8%). “Other” graduates also lean toward regional work (21.7%). Disconnection rates are somewhat higher among 4‑year graduates, but overall the trend is that **2‑year pathways support stronger early attachment to regional jobs**【749†source】.

**Year 10:** By this point, differences remain. **2‑year graduates sustain the highest regional meaningful employment (45.1%)**, while 4‑year graduates lean toward statewide meaningful employment (29.5%). “Other” graduates remain in the middle with balanced outcomes. Importantly, **no‑record rates are lowest for 2‑year graduates (21.6%)**, suggesting stronger regional retention. In contrast, 4‑year graduates have higher statewide outcomes but also higher “no record” rates (33.6%)【750†source】.

Overall, institution sector matters: **2‑year graduates show stronger long‑term regional employment**, while **4‑year graduates pursue statewide opportunities and are more mobile**, and “other” graduates fall between.

::: panel-tabset
### One year after graduating

```{r post-secondary ps.grad.InstitutionSector one year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.1, ps.grad.InstitutionSector) %>%
  filter(grad.year.1 != "After 2023") %>%
  filter(ps.grad.InstitutionSector != "Did not grad") %>%
  filter(ps.grad.InstitutionSector != "Never attended ps") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.1, var.col = data$ps.grad.InstitutionSector, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE,
         file = "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/Post-secondary/ps-grad-InstitutionSector-1.html")
```

### Five years after graduating

```{r post-secondary ps.grad.InstitutionSector five year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.5, ps.grad.InstitutionSector) %>%
  filter(grad.year.5 != "After 2023") %>%
  filter(ps.grad.InstitutionSector != "Did not grad") %>%
  filter(ps.grad.InstitutionSector != "Never attended ps") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.5, var.col = data$ps.grad.InstitutionSector, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE,
         file = "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/Post-secondary/ps-grad-InstitutionSector-5.html")
```

### Ten years after graduating

```{r post-secondary ps.grad.InstitutionSector ten year}
data <- meaningful.emp.ne %>%
  select(grad.year.10, ps.grad.InstitutionSector) %>%
  filter(grad.year.10 != "After 2023") %>%
  filter(ps.grad.InstitutionSector != "Did not grad") %>%
  filter(ps.grad.InstitutionSector != "Never attended ps") %>%
  droplevels()

tab_xtab(var.row = data$grad.year.10, var.col = data$ps.grad.InstitutionSector, 
         title = "", 
         show.exp = TRUE,
         show.col.prc = TRUE,
         file = "Data/ChatGPT/Meaningful emp ct/Post-secondary/ps-grad-InstitutionSector-10.html")
```
:::

<br>

## `highest.cred.level`

### **Key Findings**

There is a statistically significant relationship between **highest credential earned** and post‑graduation outcomes across all timeframes:

- **1 year after graduation**: χ²(20) = **25,119.08**, Cramer’s V = **0.366**, p \< .001, N = 46,777【760†source】
- **5 years after graduation**: χ²(20) = **7,371.79**, Cramer’s V = **0.231**, p \< .001, N = 34,683【761†source】
- **10 years after graduation**: χ²(12) = **1,030.86**, Cramer’s V = **0.132**, p \< .001, N = 19,828【762†source】

The association is **strong in the first year**, moderates by year 5, and weakens further by year 10, though it remains significant. The key distinction is that **higher credentials (Bachelor’s or above) delay regional employment but lead to stronger statewide employment later**, while **lower or mid‑level credentials (Associate, less than Associate) are more tied to early and sustained regional employment**.

### **Interpretation Over Time**

**Year 1:** Students with **no college** are most likely to be in regional meaningful employment (19.9%), but also heavily represented in disconnection categories (44.2% not meaningful, 30.1% no record).
